page: The OVHcloud rtx5000-28 is a KVM-based virtual server belonging to the Cloud GPU family. It features an Intel Xeon W-3235 processor with four dedicated physical cores operating at 3.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The CPU configuration lacks hyper-threading, providing exactly one thread per core. Memory capacity is 28.0 GB, yielding 7.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance is equipped with a single NVIDIA Quadro RTX 5000 GPU based on the Turing architecture, providing 16 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage consists of a 400 GB SSD, and network connectivity is provisioned with a 2 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between CPU-bound tasks and GPU-accelerated workloads. The server demonstrates top-tier performance in large language model (LLM) inference, achieving 1854.84 tokens per second for prompt processing and 67.41 tokens per second for text generation on a medium 7B model. Conversely, standard CPU and memory write operations show some limitations. Multi-core Geekbench scores and memory write bandwidth (19.59 GB/sec) fall into the lower performance tier. General CPU tasks, such as Passmark database operations and single-core stress-ng tests, perform in the average range.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the rtx5000-28 offers a specialized hardware profile that balances a dedicated professional-grade GPU with moderate CPU and memory resources. This makes it a cost-effective choice for workloads that heavily offload computation to the GPU rather than relying on massive multi-threaded CPU power. Recommended workloads include small to medium LLM inference, GPU-accelerated rendering, machine learning prototyping, and data science tasks that fit within the 16 GB VRAM and 28 GB system memory boundaries.
description: The OVHcloud rtx5000-28 is a KVM-based virtual server in the Cloud GPU family, designed for workloads requiring dedicated graphics acceleration. It features an Intel Xeon W-3235 processor with 4 dedicated physical cores running at 3.3 GHz, 28.0 GB of system RAM, and a 400 GB local SSD. Graphics and parallel computing tasks are handled by a single NVIDIA Quadro RTX 5000 GPU with 16 GB of VRAM. While CPU-centric benchmarks like multi-core Geekbench and memory write speeds are relatively modest, the server excels in GPU-driven tasks, delivering top-tier performance in LLM inference for small and medium models. This hardware profile is highly suited for machine learning inference, model prototyping, and GPU-accelerated data processing where dedicated VRAM is required without the need for massive multi-threaded CPU configurations.
og_description: OVHcloud rtx5000-28 is a KVM-based GPU server featuring an Intel Xeon W-3235 CPU, 28 GB RAM, a 400 GB SSD, and an NVIDIA Quadro RTX 5000 GPU with 16 GB VRAM, optimized for machine learning and inference workloads.
meta_description: OVHcloud rtx5000-28 is a KVM GPU server with 4 dedicated vCPUs, 28 GB RAM, a 400 GB SSD, and a 16 GB NVIDIA Quadro RTX 5000 GPU.
tagline: A dedicated GPU instance balancing an NVIDIA Turing accelerator with dedicated Intel cores for efficient machine learning inference.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon W-3235 processor with 4 dedicated physical cores at 3.3 GHz
Dedicated NVIDIA Quadro RTX 5000 GPU with 16 GB of Turing VRAM
28.0 GB system memory and 400 GB of local SSD storage
Top-tier performance in small and medium LLM inference workloads
2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with one public IPv4 address
Best suited for machine learning prototyping, rendering, and data science
